qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Nil pointer dereference on unauthenticated data-change-notification callback endpoint causes denial of service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes improper error handling in the PATCH handler for sdm-subscriptions where a downstream 400 from UDR is converted to 500, plus PATCH-to-PUT method translation. This finding describes a nil pointer dereference in the POST callback handler for /sdm-subscriptions due to a missing :supi route parameter. Different HTTP method (POST vs PATCH), different handler (callback handler in api_httpcallback.go vs SDM PATCH handler), and different root cause (nil dereference from missing route param vs improper error handling of downstream response).

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-side request forgery via unvalidated AMF deregistration callback URL in UE context management
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes improper error handling (CWE-209) in the UDM SDM PATCH handler for sdm-subscriptions. This finding describes an SSRF vulnerability in the UECM registration endpoints (PUT /nudm-uecm/v1/:ueId/registrations/amf-3gpp-access). Different service (UECM vs SDM), different endpoint, different vulnerability class (SSRF vs improper error handling), and different root cause (missing URL validation on DeregCallbackUri vs error handling converting 400 to 500).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory describes improper error handling in the PATCH handler for sdm-subscriptions in the UDM SDM service, where a downstream 400 Bad Request from UDR is incorrectly converted to 500 Internal Server Error, combined with PATCH-to-PUT method translation (CWE-209). Finding 1 (nil-deref-unauthenticated-callback) targets the POST callback handler for /sdm-subscriptions with a nil dereference root cause due to missing :supi route parameter — a different handler, HTTP method, and root cause. Finding 2 (ssrf-via-amf-deregistration-callback) targets the UECM registration endpoints with an SSRF via unvalidated DeregCallbackUri — a completely different service, endpoint, and vulnerability class.
evidence: Advisory: PATCH /sdm-subscriptions with empty supi, UDM forwards PUT to UDR, UDR returns 400, UDM converts to 500 (CWE-209)
Finding 1: POST /sdm-subscriptions callback handler, nil dereference from missing :supi route param (different handler/method/root cause)
Finding 2: PUT /nudm-uecm/v1/:ueId/registrations/amf-3gpp-access, SSRF via DeregCallbackUri (different service/endpoint/vulnerability class)
